industries.name: Stainless Steel
uses: Nickel stabilizes the austenitic (face-centered cubic) crystal structure of 300-series stainless steels — 304 (8% Ni), 316 (10% Ni), 310 (19% Ni) — giving them toughness, ductility, and formability versus ferritic grades

Austenitic stainless steel is non-magnetic, readily weldable, and maintains ductility at cryogenic temperatures (-200°C) — making it essential for LNG storage, cryogenic vessels, and food processing equipment
Duplex stainless steels (2205: 5% Ni, 2507: 7% Ni) combine austenitic and ferritic phases for high strength and chloride stress corrosion resistance in offshore oil, chemical plant, and desalination
Superaustenitic grades (254 SMO, AL-6XN: 18–25% Ni) for extreme seawater and acidic service in pulp bleaching, seawater heat exchangers, and FGD scrubber systems
Nickel alloying also used in maraging steels (18% Ni) for ultra-high-strength tool steel, pressure vessel plate, and rocket motor casings — nickel enables age-hardening without carbon

companies.name: Norilsk Nickel (Nornickel)
companies.use: World's largest nickel and palladium producer. Operates smelters at Norilsk, Siberia and Kola Peninsula from sulfide ore deposits. Major supplier of Class 1 nickel to stainless steel, battery, and specialty alloy industries globally.

companies.name: Vale
companies.use: World's second-largest nickel producer. Operates Ontario (Sudbury), Manitoba (Thompson), and Caledonia (Voisey's Bay) sulfide mines in Canada, and Onça Puma laterite operation in Brazil. Major supplier of nickel pellets and powder.

companies.name: Glencore
companies.use: Major nickel producer from operations in Canada (Sudbury), Australia (Murrin Murrin), and New Caledonia (Koniambo). Supplies nickel cathode, briquettes, and ferronickel to stainless steel mills and battery manufacturers.

companies.name: Tsingshan Holding Group
companies.use: World's largest stainless steel and nickel pig iron (NPI) producer. Pioneered NPI technology — using lower-grade Indonesian laterite ore in rotary kiln-electric furnace (RKEF) process — that collapsed nickel prices in 2021.

industries.name: EV Batteries & Energy Storage
industries.share: ~12% of nickel use — fastest growing segment
uses: NMC (nickel manganese cobalt) cathodes: NMC 811 contains ~48% nickel by cathode weight. Higher nickel content increases energy density (range) but requires more careful thermal management. Used by LG, SK, Panasonic
NCA (nickel cobalt aluminum oxide): ~80% Ni. Tesla's preferred chemistry in 4680 cells and Model 3/Y Long Range — maximum energy density per kilogram for long-range EVs
Class 1 nickel (>99.8% purity, in forms of cathode, briquette, or sulfate) required for battery manufacturing — LME-grade nickel or nickel sulfate dissolved from Class 1 products
Nickel sulfate (NiSO₄·6H₂O) is the actual precursor for battery cathode manufacturing — produced by dissolving nickel metal or precipitating from leach solution of nickel laterite (HPAL process)
Indonesia dominates emerging nickel-for-batteries supply chain: HPAL (high-pressure acid leach) plants process laterite ore to mixed hydroxide precipitate (MHP) → nickel sulfate → battery cathode

companies.name: Panasonic Energy
companies.use: Major NCA battery cell manufacturer and Tesla's primary cell supplier. Operates Gigafactory Nevada jointly with Tesla. Its 4680 cells use high-nickel NCA cathode chemistry requiring battery-grade nickel sulfate.

companies.name: LG Energy Solution
companies.use: Second-largest EV battery manufacturer. Produces high-nickel NMC 811 and NCMA (adding aluminum) cells for GM Ultium, Volkswagen, Hyundai/Kia, and Stellantis electric vehicles.

companies.name: PT Vale Indonesia
companies.use: Operates Sorowako nickel-in-matte smelter in Sulawesi, Indonesia — one of the world's largest nickel operations. Indonesia now hosts the world's largest nickel reserves and is rapidly developing HPAL refining for battery-grade nickel sulfate.

industries.name: Superalloys & Specialty Alloys
uses: Nickel-based superalloys (Inconel 718, René 88DT, CMSX-4 single crystal) operate at 85–90% of their melting point in jet engine turbine sections — no other material class approaches this capability
Inconel 625 and 825 for seawater and acidic service: subsea umbilicals, chemical injection lines, flue gas desulfurization scrubbers, and heat exchanger tubes in highly corrosive environments
Monel (Ni-Cu alloys) for seawater piping, pump shafts, and marine hardware — excellent corrosion resistance in reducing and hydrofluoric acid environments
Invar (64% Ni-Fe): near-zero thermal expansion coefficient used in precision instruments, CRT shadow masks, LNG membrane tanks, and composite tooling masters
Permalloy and Mu-metal (high-Ni soft magnetic alloys) for magnetic shielding of sensitive instruments, audio transformers, and data storage read heads

companies.name: Precision Castparts (Berkshire Hathaway)
companies.use: World's largest manufacturer of nickel superalloy investment castings and forgings. Produces turbine blades, vanes, discs, and structural castings for GE Aviation, Pratt & Whitney, Rolls-Royce, and Safran engines.

companies.name: Special Metals (PCC)
companies.use: Inventor of Inconel, Incoloy, Nimonic, and Udimet nickel superalloy families. Produces wrought nickel alloy bar, sheet, and plate for aerospace, chemical processing, and power generation applications. Owned by Precision Castparts.

companies.name: ATI (Allegheny Technologies)
companies.use: Major producer of nickel-based superalloy billet, bar, and forgings for jet engine rotating components. Supplies Inconel 718, Waspaloy, and René-series alloy forgings to engine OEMs and Tier 1 aerospace forgers.

industries.name: Electroplating & Surface Finishing
uses: Electroplated nickel as corrosion-resistant undercoat beneath chromium decorative plating — nickel provides the corrosion barrier; thin chrome layer provides the bright appearance on automotive trim, plumbing, and hardware

Electroless nickel plating: auto-catalytic deposition of Ni-P or Ni-B alloy deposits uniform coating inside bores, on complex shapes, and on non-conductive substrates — used for hardening bearing surfaces, molds, and precision components
Nickel sulfamate plating for dimensional restoration of worn aerospace components and electroforming of precision nickel parts such as CD/DVD stampers and rocket motor nozzle components
Bright nickel plating on steel parts: bicycle components, tools, kitchen fixtures, and consumer electronics hardware — combining corrosion protection with aesthetic finish
Nickel strike layers on aluminum and stainless steel before further plating — provides adhesion for subsequent copper, chrome, or gold electrodeposits on otherwise difficult-to-plate substrates

companies.name: Atotech (MKS Instruments)
companies.use: World's largest specialty plating chemistry supplier. Provides nickel sulfamate, nickel sulfate, and nickel acetate formulations for decorative, functional, and electronics plating applications globally.

companies.name: MacDermid Enthone (Element Solutions)
companies.use: Major supplier of electroless nickel, bright nickel, and nickel alloy plating chemistries for automotive, aerospace, electronics, and general metal finishing applications worldwide.
